nativeRange.habitat: Found in inshore reef environments, where it is commonly observed over sloping rubble bottoms and shallow rubble flats.
nativeRange.biotope: Western Pacific and Melanesian reefs, reported across a wide depth range from roughly 5 to 70 m.

diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis and brine shrimp, finely chopped meaty seafood, and high-quality marine flake or pellets. Commonly advised to be fed 2–3 small meals per day, especially for newly arrived specimens; as a small planktivorous wrasse, it tends to benefit from frequent feeding rather than one large daily meal.

avoidWith: other male fairy wrasses in the same tank

summary: A small, hardy Western Pacific fairy wrasse from the rubble flats of Melanesia, generally considered peaceful and reef-safe, and well suited to mature mid-sized reef aquariums with a secure lid.
careNotes: Conde's Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus condei) is a small fairy wrasse from the Western Pacific, originally described from Papua New Guinea and commonly collected from Melanesia, Indonesia, and the Philippines for the aquarium trade. Unlike some rare collector species in the genus, it tends to appear with reasonable regularity through online marine fish retailers. Generally considered peaceful and reef-safe; nipping at corals or ornamental invertebrates is not commonly reported. Like most Cirrhilabrus species, it tends to be a notable jumper, and a tightly fitted lid or eurobrace is strongly advised. It is best kept as a single male, or as a male with one or more females introduced at the same time to form a small harem — adding females after the male is already established tends to provoke aggression.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species page used for adult size (~3 in / 7.6 cm), 50–55 gal tank recommendation, water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), 'easy' care level, peaceful and reef-safe rating, carnivorous diet, jumping/lid warning, and harem-keeping guidance.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed max length 8.0 cm TL, family Labridae, order Eupercaria, depth range 5–70 m, distribution Western Pacific (Papua New Guinea), distinct pairing during breeding. IUCN: Least Concern. FishBase wild-temperature range (~27–29°C) is warmer than the LiveAquaria aquarium recommendation (22–26°C); we have kept the hobby-standard reef temperature band rather than the warmer wild value.
